# Robot Framework Integration

`testid gen-locators` reads a registry and writes one Python module per component containing XPath constants:

```python
# tests/locators/order_list.py - Generated by testid-gen-locators
# Component: order-list

orderList_table_auftragsliste = "xpath://*[@data-testid='order-list__table--auftragsliste']"  # testid-managed
orderList_th_id               = "xpath://*[@data-testid='order-list__th--id']"  # testid-managed
orderList_th_kunde            = "xpath://*[@data-testid='order-list__th--kunde']"  # testid-managed
```

## The `# testid-managed` marker

Every generated line ends with `# testid-managed`. A companion Python replacer can use the marker to update constant names automatically when `testid diff` reports a rename.

## Variable name template

The Python variable name is built from the template in `locators.variableFormat`, using the same placeholder syntax as the tagger's `idFormat`. Default:

```json
"locators": {
  "variableFormat": "{component}_{element}_{key}"
}
```

Examples with the default template:

| Registry entry | Variable name |
|---|---|
| `component=order-list`, `element=dropdown`, `key=customer` | `orderList_dropdown_customer` |
| `component=login`, `element=input`, `key=email` | `login_input_email` |
| hash-only testid, `component=order-list`, `element=button`, `key=submit` | `orderList_button_submit` |

The variable name is reconstructed from the registry entry, not parsed from the testid string - so hash-only testids still produce readable Python constants.

## Template options

| Template | Example output | Use case |
|---|---|---|
| `{component}_{element}_{key}` (default) | `orderList_dropdown_customer` | General-purpose. |
| `{element}_{key}` | `dropdown_customer` | Component is obvious from the module filename. |
| `{component}_{key}` | `orderList_customer` | Element type not needed in the name. |
| `loc_{component}_{element}_{key}` | `loc_orderList_dropdown_customer` | Prefix for IDE autocomplete grouping. |
| `{component}_{element}_{key}_{hash}` | `orderList_dropdown_customer_a1b2c3` | Disambiguation when semantics would collide. |

### CLI override

```bash
testid gen-locators testids.latest.json --out-dir tests/locators \
  --variable-format '{element}_{key}'
```

## Merge mode

Since v0.5.0, `gen-locators` preserves manual edits by default. On every re-run it:

- Reads the existing target file.
- Replaces only lines ending in `# testid-managed`.
- Drops managed lines whose testid is no longer in the registry.
- Appends new registry entries to the last managed block.
- Leaves everything without the marker untouched (custom locators, helper constants, comments, imports, blank lines).

Example - a file with one manual helper between two generated lines:

```python
# tests/locators/login.py
login_input_email    = "xpath://*[@data-testid='login__input--email']"  # testid-managed
login_custom_debug   = "xpath://div[@id='debug']"
login_button_signIn  = "xpath://*[@data-testid='login__button--sign-in']"  # testid-managed
```

After the next `testid gen-locators`, `login_custom_debug` stays exactly where it is. The two managed lines get rebuilt from the registry, preserving variable-name changes or selector updates.

### Mode options

| Mode | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `merge` (default) | Preserve manual lines, replace `# testid-managed` lines. |
| `overwrite` | Discard any prior content, rewrite the file from scratch. |
| `refuse` | Fail if the target file already exists. |

Set via config (`locators.mode`) or CLI flag (`--mode <value>`). The legacy `--no-overwrite` flag is a deprecated alias for `--mode refuse`.

### Rules inside a managed block

- New registry entries append to the last managed block.
- Managed lines are sorted alphabetically by variable name within each block.
- A user-edited selector that still carries the marker is overwritten - the marker means the line is owned by the tool.
- A comment or blank line inside what was previously a managed block splits it into two blocks. The comment is preserved as a manual line.

## Usage in Robot Framework

```robot
*** Settings ***
Library    SeleniumLibrary
Variables  ../locators/order_list.py

*** Test Cases ***
Order list is visible
    Wait Until Element Is Visible    ${orderList_table_auftragsliste}
```
